caption:
4 CSS container query patterns that replace media queries.

The component knows its own width now. No more guessing breakpoints based on the viewport.

Every major browser supports this since 2023.

📌 SAVE this — you'll need it.

Are you using container queries yet? 👇

#css #webdev #frontend #coding #programming #developer #responsive #containerqueries #codingtips #webdesign

slides.title: CSS container queries that replace media queries
slides.subtitle: The component knows its own width. No viewport guessing.

slides.title: @container — the basics
slides.desc: Style children based on the parent's size, not the viewport.

slides.title: Named containers — target specific parents
slides.desc: Name your containers so nested components know which parent to query.
slides.punchline: No more fighting with viewport breakpoints.

slides.title: Container query units — cqw, cqh
slides.desc: Like vw/vh but relative to the container, not the viewport.

slides.title: Style queries — query CSS values
slides.desc: Check a container's computed styles, not just its size.
slides.punchline: CSS variables as media queries. Wild.

slides.title: Container Queries Cheat Sheet
slides.question: Which pattern are you using first? 👇

slides.teaser: Next: CSS :has() — the selector that killed JavaScript
